structs.description: Audio data is stored in the `data` variable as a `List[MFloat[Self.num_chans]]` where each `MFloat[Self.num_chans]` represents a single frame of audio data for all channels. For example, if `num_chans` is 2, each element of `data` would be an `MFloat[2]` where the first element is the sample value for the left channel and the second element is the sample value for the right channel.

args.description: A `List` of `List`s of `Float64` representing the audio data for each channel.
args.name: data

args.description: The sample rate of the audio data.
args.name: sample_rate

overloads.name: __init__
overloads.signature: __init__(out self, data: List[SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans]], sample_rate: Float64)

args.description: Sample rate of the buffer.
args.name: sample_rate

args.name: file_name

args.description: Number of wavetables per channel. This is only used if the sound file being loaded contains multiple wavetables concatenated in a single channel.
args.name: num_wavetables

args.description: Whether to print verbose output.
args.name: verbose

overloads.name: load
overloads.signature: load(file_name: String, num_wavetables: Int = 1, verbose: Bool = False) -> Self
overloads.summary: Initialize a SIMDBuffer by loading data from a WAV file using SciPy and NumPy.

structs.description:
`SpanInterpolator` supports various interpolation methods including

* no interpolation (none)
* linear interpolation
* quadratic interpolation
* cubic interpolation
* lagrange interpolation (4th order)
* sinc interpolation

The available interpolation methods are defined in the struct [Interp](MMMWorld.md#struct-interp).

overloads.name: idx_in_range
overloads.signature: idx_in_range[num_chans: Int = 1](data: Span[SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans], origin], idx: Int) -> Bool

args.name: world

args.description: The `Span[MFloat[num_chans]]` to read from.
args.name: data

args.description: The floating-point index to read at.
args.name: f_idx

args.description: The previous floating-point index (used for [SincInterpolation](SincInterpolator.md)).
args.name: prev_f_idx

parameters.description: Number of channels in the data.
parameters.name: num_chans

parameters.description: Interpolation method to use (from [Interp](MMMWorld.md#struct-interp) enum).
parameters.name: interp

parameters.description: Whether to wrap indices that go out of bounds.
parameters.name: bWrap

parameters.description: Bitmask for wrapping indices (if applicable). If 0, standard modulo wrapping is used. If non-zero, bitwise AND wrapping is used (only valid for power-of-two lengths).
parameters.name: mask
